case_studies.name: Slack
case_studies.summary: Messaging platform that became essential workplace communication tool
case_studies.key_insight: Originally internal tool for game company. Found 10x better use case in communication.
critical_mistakes_avoided: Didn't build horizontal platform too early
case_studies.market_conditions: Cloud computing, chat becoming popular, team remote work accelerating
lessons: Bottom-up adoption powerful
Network effects defensible moat
Free tier key for adoption
Customer obsession matters

case_studies.name: Theranos
case_studies.summary: Blood testing startup that made false claims about technology capabilities
case_studies.key_insight: Ambition without technology foundation led to fraud and collapse
case_studies.business_model: B2B Healthcare - Direct to consumer blood tests
key_failure_factors: Lack of scientific rigor and peer review
case_studies.market_conditions: Healthcare was hot, investment frothy, no independent verification
lessons: Technology must actually work
Transparency builds trust
Peer review and rigor essential for medical
Founder fallibility important
Culture that allows dissent prevents disasters

case_studies.name: Figma
case_studies.summary: Collaborative design tool that replaced desktop software (Adobe, Sketch)
case_studies.key_insight: Identified shift from desktop to web. Built collaborative, browser-based design tool.
key_success_factors: Timing: Web tech matured for real-time collaboration
Strong design of the product itself
critical_mistakes_avoided: Didn't try to compete on desktop (used web as advantage)
case_studies.market_conditions: Browser tech (WebGL) matured, remote work growing, design gaining importance
lessons: Timing technology adoption cycles critical
Collaboration is powerful differentiator
Web can compete with desktop with right approach
Design of the product matters for design tools
Patient capital allows longer runway

case_studies.name: Zenefits
case_studies.summary: HR benefits platform that grew fast but had compliance and regulatory problems
case_studies.key_insight: Hypergrowth masked regulatory and compliance issues in licensed industry
key_failure_factors: Moved into regulated industry (insurance broker) without proper licensing
Valuation at odds with actual business
case_studies.market_conditions: HR tech hot, frothy investment market, regulatory capture not evident
lessons: Regulated industries need regulatory expertise from day 1
Metrics (growth) don't matter if business is illegal
Startup culture of 'move fast' incompatible with licensing
Board and CEO need regulatory savvy
Compliance is not overhead, is business model

case_studies.name: Stripe
case_studies.summary: Payment processor that made accepting payments simple for businesses and developers
case_studies.key_insight: Identified that payment processing was unnecessarily hard. Built developer-friendly API.
critical_mistakes_avoided: Didn't aim for enterprise sales initially
Listened to customer feedback on product
case_studies.market_conditions: Online commerce accelerating, fragmented payment ecosystem, developer empowerment
lessons: Developer tools have strong moat
API design and documentation matter hugely
Self-serve adoption faster than sales
Quality and consistency compound
Picking large underserved market matters

case_studies.name: Quibi
case_studies.summary: Short-form video platform launched at wrong time with wrong model
case_studies.key_insight: Great execution on wrong idea at wrong time (launched when COVID lockdowns made short-form irrelevant)
key_failure_factors: Launched during COVID (mobile use case invalid)
Couldn't achieve product-market fit in time
warning_signs: Mobile-only turned out to be limiting
case_studies.market_conditions: COVID-19 pandemic (shift to home entertainment), TikTok dominance, creator economy maturing
lessons: Timing product launches for market conditions
Execution and capital alone don't guarantee success
Network effects and communities (TikTok) beat content budgets
Market research should have revealed issues
Sometimes the idea is right but timing is wrong
